name: Real Estate, Education And Community Housing, Inc.
description_short: HUD-approved nonprofit providing free homebuyer education, foreclosure counseling, and down payment assistance to low-to-moderate income families in Florida since 2006.
description_long:
Real Estate, Education and Community Housing, Inc. (R.E.A.C.H.) is a Florida-based 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization founded in January 2006 and approved by HUD as a Foreclosure and Housing Counseling Agency. The organization is affiliated with HomeFree-USA and operates with a mission to help low-to-moderate income individuals and families achieve homeownership through education, counseling, and financial assistance.

R.E.A.C.H. offers a comprehensive range of free and low-cost services including HUD-approved homebuyer education workshops (both virtual and in-person), foreclosure prevention and eviction counseling, down payment assistance programs, credit counseling, money management education, and pre-purchase counseling. They deliver workshops in multiple languages whenever possible and use NeighborWorks curriculum for homebuyer education classes. The organization also facilitates connections with local mortgage lenders, real estate professionals, and attorneys to support participants in their homeownership journey.

The organization distinguishes itself through its focus on underserved populations and its comprehensive approach combining financial education with practical assistance. R.E.A.C.H. offers specialized programs including foreclosure intervention, a Credit Mitigation Program with potential grant assistance (Second Chance Grants), and post-closing home maintenance classes approved by the City of West Palm Beach. Their 8-hour homebuyer workshops are available in multiple formats and languages, demonstrating accessibility as a core value.

As a nonprofit, R.E.A.C.H. operates entirely on a free-help model with no predatory lending or hidden fees. The main limitation is geographic focus (Florida-based operations) and reliance on grants and donations for funding, which may affect program availability. Their website emphasizes education and prevention rather than quick fixes, making them suitable for families serious about long-term financial stability and homeownership rather than those seeking immediate cash solutions.
pros: HUD-approved nonprofit with no conflicts of interest—genuinely serving low-income populations rather than profiting from their financial distress
Offers free 8-hour homebuyer education workshops in multiple languages and delivery formats (virtual and in-person)
Provides foreclosure prevention and eviction counseling specifically designed to keep families in their homes
Credit Mitigation Program includes Second Chance Grants to pay off past-due accounts for pre-qualified individuals
NeighborWorks-curriculum based homebuyer education meets rigorous standards and local government requirements
Facilitates connections with legitimate mortgage lenders, real estate professionals, and attorneys—not loan sharks
Multi-language support (Arabic, Chinese, Dutch, French, German, Italian, Portuguese, Russian, Spanish) demonstrates genuine accessibility
cons: Geographic limitation: Florida-based organization, limiting accessibility for out-of-state consumers
No indication of online counseling for remote participants outside their service area
Funding dependent on donations and grants, which may affect consistency and expansion of services
Website lacks specific information about down payment assistance program limits, income thresholds, or approval rates
No mention of credit monitoring or identity theft protection services that some competitors offer
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Florida seeking free education and guidance before purchasing
Homeowners facing foreclosure or eviction who need HUD-approved counseling and intervention strategies
Low-to-moderate income families working to rebuild credit and qualify for mortgages
Spanish-speaking and multilingual individuals who need homeownership support in their native language
diagnosis: R.E.A.C.H. is best for Florida residents serious about achieving sustainable homeownership who need education, credit rebuilding, or foreclosure prevention from a legitimate nonprofit. The main caveat is that this is not a quick-fix lender—they emphasize long-term financial stability through education and counseling, making them ideal for motivated families but not for those seeking immediate cash or loans.
services: Free 8-hour Homebuyer Education Workshops (virtual and in-person)
HUD-approved foreclosure prevention and eviction counseling
Pre-purchase homebuyer counseling and intake assessments
Down payment assistance program guidance and qualification help
Credit Mitigation Program with budgeting and savings assistance
Second Chance Grants to pay off past-due accounts (pre-qualified applicants)
Money management and financial wellness education
HELOC and reverse mortgage information and counseling
Referral intake forms and community partnerships
Multilingual support and culturally responsive programming
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
